questions.question_text: Simplify: $$\left[\left(\frac{16}{36}\div\frac{4}{7}\right)\times\frac{14}{6}+\frac{5}{12}\right]\div\frac{39}{12}\div\frac{12}{7}$$
Which of the following ratios is equal to 3:2?
If the interest on a sum of \u20b9y at 4% per annum compounded annually for 2 years is \u20b9780, then the total amount payable at the end of the two-year period will be _______________

A pair of shoes is marked at \u20b92800 and is subject to a trade discount of 18%. Under a special offer, a customer buys 2 pairs at the discounted price and gets an additional 2 pairs at 50% off the discounted price. Find the total cost to the customer for 7 pairs of shoes.
Three numbers are in the ratio 3 : 7 : 2, and their LCM is 8820. Their HCF is:

Pipes A and B together can fill a cistern in 10 hours. Pipes B and C together can fill the same cistern in 20 hours, and pipes C and A together can fill it in 12 hours. Find the time taken by pipe B alone to fill the cistern.

A spherical water tank has an internal diameter of 10 meters. If the tank is coated with a material that costs \u20b95 per square meter, what is the total cost to coat the entire internal surface? (Use $$\pi = 3.14$$)

The average monthly income of six employees in an office is \u20b924,000. When a new employee joins the office, the average income of all seven employees becomes \u20b925,500. What is the monthly income of the new employee?

In a college, the ratio of science to arts students is different for each of its three batches. In Batch A, the ratio is 5:3, in Batch B it is 7:4, and in Batch C it is 3:5. Which batch has the highest proportion of science students?
If the third proportional to 4 and 20 is m and the third proportional to m and 50 is n, what is the value of n?
In $$\triangle XYZ$$ $$\angle Y = \angle XZ$$ and $$\angle X = 40^{\circ}$$ what is the measure of $$\angle Z$$?
The mean of the following data is 12.5. Find the missing frequency p.Data values (x): 10, 15Frequency (f): 5, p
A delivery van covers the first 100 km at 50 km/h, the next 150 km at 60 km/h, and the last 50 km at 40 km/h. What is the average speed for the entire journey (correct to two places of decimal)?

The population of a town increases by 10% in the year 2015 and decreases by 15% in the year 2016. If the population of the town at the end of the year 2016 is 1,87,000, find the population of the town in the beginning of the year 2015.
Which of the following numbers is divisible by 11?
In a division sum, the divisor is 14 times the quotient and 5 times the remainder. If the remainder is 86, what is the dividend?

Sangeeta's salary is revised twice in two consecutive years. In the first year, the salary is increased by 12%. In the second year, it is decreased by 10%. Despite this, her salary after the second revision is \u20b91,584 more than the original salary. What is Sangeeta's original monthly salary (in \u20b9)?
A train covers a distance of 300 kilometers in 5 hours. If the same train takes 6 hours to cover the same distance, what is the change in its speed?
Simplify: $$(0.25\times1.6)\div(0.04\times0.5)$$
A and B can do a piece of work in 12 days and 30 days, respectively. Both work for 4 days and then A leaves. How long (in days) will B take to complete the remaining work?
The amount under simple interest on a sum of \u20b98,000 for 3 years at an interest rate of 7.5% per annum is _______________

If a man bought an article at 20% less than its marked price and sold it for \u20b910 more than its marked price, his profit was 40%. What was the marked price (in \u20b9) of the article (correct to two decimal places)?
The present age of Person A is 5 years more than twice the age of Person B. If Person B is 18 years old, what is the present age (in years) of Person A?

A trader buys a consignment of goods for \u20b92400. He sells one-third of the goods at a 20% profit, and one-fourth of the goods at a 10% loss, and the remaining goods at cost price. What is the overall profit or loss percentage?

A shopkeeper offers the following schemes on toys of the same marked price.(A) Successive discounts of 30% and 20% on any number of toys bought.(B) Successive discounts of 38%, 42%, and 30% on any number of toys bought.(C) 30% off on 4 toys, 40% off on 8 toys, 50% off on 10 or more toys bought.(D) 7 toys free of cost on buying 10 toys.If the shopkeeper wants to buy 10 to 20 toys, which of the above schemes is the least beneficial to her?
Eight mangoes and five oranges cost \u20b9164, while five mangoes and eight oranges cost \u20b9148. Determine the cost of three mangoes and three oranges.
A ladder leans against a wall, making an angle of $$60^{\circ}$$ with the ground. If the foot of the ladder is 16 metres from the wall, what is the length of the ladder?
Sphere A has a radius of 8 cm and Sphere B has a radius of 3 cm. What is the ratio of the surface area of A to that of B?

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a correct match of a climate type with its vegetation or features as per Koeppen's scheme of classification of climate?

Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements.Statement: All Chickens are Quails. All Quails are Pigeons.Conclusion (I): No Pigeons are Chickens.Conclusion (II): Some Pigeons are Chickens.
Which of the following letter-number clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete?EIJ 33 GKL 27 IMN 21 KOP 15 ?
In a certain code language, 'jaw bush over' is coded as 'ht vg xe' and 'zebra egg jaw' is coded as 'vg db la'. How is 'jaw' coded in the given language?

Seven people, J, K, C, D, E, G and H, are sitting in a row, facing north.D sits second to the right of H. One person sits between the right of E. K is the immediate neighbour of J and E. H sits third to the right of D.How many people sit between C and K?
Select the pair which follows the same pattern as that followed by the two sets of pairs given below. Both pairs follow the same pattern.GJF : LAE LGP : QRU
FR 6 is related to JL 17 in a certain way. In the same way, EU 12 is related to IO 23. To which of the following is SL 3 related, following the same logic?

Refer to the given series and answer the question that follows. All numbers are single-digit numbers only. Counting to be done from left to right only.(Left) 2 8 7 5 6 9 7 6 8 4 8 2 1 8 2 8 6 8 1 3 1 1 3 (Right)How many such odd digits are there, each of which is immediately preceded by an odd digit and also immediately followed by an even digit?

Moksh starts from Point A and drives 21 km towards the south. He then takes a left turn, drives 13 km, turns left, and drives 28 km. He then takes a left turn and drives 21 km. He takes a final left turn, drives 8 km and stops at Point P. How far (shortest distance) and in which new direction would he have to drive in order to reach Point A again? (All turns are 90-degree turns only, unless specified.)
If 2 is added to each odd digit and 2 is subtracted from each even digit in the number 6431782, then what will be difference between the highest and lowest digits in the number thus formed?

Each letter in the word MONKEY is arranged in the English alphabetical order. How many letters are there in the English alphabetical order between the new letter cluster thus formed?the fourth from the right in the new letter cluster and the one which is fourth from the right in the new letter cluster thus formed?
If '2' is added to each odd digit and '1' is subtracted from each even digit in the number 2568794, then how many digits will appear more than once in the new number thus formed?
If '+' means '', '' means '', '' means '', and '' means '', then what will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation?$17 - 3 + 8 \div 21 \times 3 = ?$
In a certain code language, "MIAQ" is coded as "40" and "TNCR" is coded as "47".What is the code for "PXAJ" in that given language?

Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to the group?(Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)

Seven people, R, P, Y, J, E, F and N, are sitting in a row, facing north.Y sits third from the right end. Only three people sit between R and Y. N is the immediate neighbour of E and J. F sits second to the right of E.How many people sit between R and J?
Anil ranked 27th from the top and 19th from the bottom in his class. How many students are there in his class?

Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group?(Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)

In a row of people all facing the north, Jonny is 24th from the left end. Anuj is 20th from the left end. Anuj is exactly between Jonny and Rishabh. If Rishabh is 16th from the right end of the line, how many people are there in the row?

In a certain code language,A $$\times$$ B means 'A is the son of B'A - B means 'A is the brother of B'A @ B means 'A is the brother of B'A + B means 'A is the daughter of B'Based on the above, how is P related to X if 'P - Q @ S $$\times$$ T $$\times$$ X'?
What should come in place of '?' in the given series?92 92 85 85 78 78 ?
Which of the following letter-number clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete?ICX 9 LZX 16, OIB 25, RLD 36, ?

In a certain code language,A $$\times$$ P means 'A is the father of B'.A - P means 'A is the mother of B'.A + P means 'A is the daughter of B' andA $$\div$$ P means 'A is the sister of B'.Based on the above, how is C related to M if 'C $$\div$$ D + E $$\times$$ L $$\times$$ M'?

Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets.(Note: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g., 13 operations on 13 and not on 1 and 3. You may assume that the operations will be performed on the given numbers only. )adding/subtracting/multiplying to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.

Each letter in the word JUMBLED is arranged in the English alphabetical order. How many letters are there in the English alphabetical order between the letter which is fourth from the left and the one which is first from the right in the new letter cluster thus formed?
What should come in place of '?' in the given series?65 ? 24 15 11 10
What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete?MV7 PT13 RR19 ?

Based on the alphabetical order, three of the following four are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group?(Note: The odd man out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter cluster.)

Read the given statement(s) and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statement(s) is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s).Statements:All carpets are shoes.All paintings are shoes.Conclusions:(I) Some carpets are paintings.(II) Some shoes are paintings.

Seven boxes A, B, C, D, E, F and G are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only two boxes are kept between C and G. Only F is kept above B. No box is kept below G. E is kept at some place below A but at some place above D. How many boxes are kept between F and D?

SMAL is related to VQFR in a certain way based on the English alphabetical order. In the same way, ECUJ is related to HGZP. To which of the following options is NOJB related, following the same logic?
